commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d59d07c3ee072e28415b592f553e7fecaba9bfbe)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` to the shared Google Gemini static catalog used by the Google and Google Vertex providers.

PR surface: Source +9. Total +9 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main lacks the `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` static catalog row while the model resolver has a clear `model_not_found` path when no matching provider model row exists. I did not run a live Google Vertex request in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and a small upstream-contract metadata correction before normal maintainer review can proceed.

target.title: fix(google): add missing gemini-3.1-flash-lite to google-vertex catalog
target.body:
## Problem

Configuring the model `google-vertex/gemini-3.1-flash-lite` results in a `ProviderFailoverError` because the model ID is missing from the static catalog definition.

Error message:
```
Found agents.defaults.models["google-vertex/gemini-3.1-flash-lite"], but no matching
models.providers["google-vertex"].models[] entry.
Add { "id": "gemini-3.1-flash-lite" } to models.providers...
```

## Root Cause

The `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` model was released by Google on May 7, 2026, but it was never added to the shared `GOOGLE_GEMINI_TEXT_MODELS` array in `extensions/google/provider-catalog.ts`. Because both the `google` and `google-vertex` providers (and `google-gemini-cli`) consume this same array, the model is unavailable across all Google Gemini providers.

## Fix

Add the missing model definition to the shared catalog array:
- id: `gemini-3.1-flash-lite`
- reasoning: `true` (Google Flash Lite supports thinking levels)
- contextWindow: `1_048_576`
- maxTokens: `65_536`

## Real Behavior Proof

### Environment
- OS: Linux (Ubuntu 22.04)
- Node.js: v24.14.1
- Commit tested: `d59d07c3ee0`

### Reproduction
1. **Before the fix**, check the static catalog array:
   ```bash
   grep -n "gemini-3.1-flash-lite" extensions/google/provider-catalog.ts
   ```
   Result: **no matches** (model missing).

2. **After the fix**, check again:
   ```bash
   grep -n "gemini-3.1-flash-lite" extensions/google/provider-catalog.ts
   ```
   Result: lines 46-54 show the model definition present.

### Verification
- The model ID `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` is now listed alongside `gemini-3.1-pro-preview` and `gemini-3-flash-preview` in the catalog.
- The existing `modelIdNormalization` map in `provider-models.ts` already has an alias from `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview` → `gemini-3.1-flash-lite`, which will now work correctly because the target model exists in the catalog.

Fixes #89390
target.author: xuzehan-icenter
